question.text: What penalty was imposed on Shreepati Holdings & Finance Pvt. Ltd. for AML/CFT violations?

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Shreepati was penalized Rs. 3 lakh under Section 15HB of SEBI Act for: (1) delayed AML policy update (8 months), (2) closing all 137 exchange alerts without documentation, (3) no system to monitor transactions against client financials, and (4) rectifying deficiencies only after Show Cause Notice, not after inspection.
**The Trap:** Rs. 40 lakh was the penalty in the BOI Shareholding case (reduced from Rs. 40 lakh to Rs. 6 lakh on appeal). The Shreepati penalty of Rs. 3 lakh is moderate, reflecting the nature of violations.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Compare penalties across cases: Way2Wealth = Rs. 1 lakh, SKSE = Rs. 2 lakh, Raima = Rs. 2 lakh, Shreepati = Rs. 3 lakh, Marfatia = Nil. The severity correlates with the gravity and duration of non-compliance.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The escalating penalty pattern shows SEBI considers: (1) gravity of violation, (2) duration of non-compliance, (3) whether corrections were made promptly, and (4) impact on investor interest. Corrective action taken ONLY after SCN (as Shreepati did) attracts higher penalties than proactive correction.
